failure_modes.text: A shortcut can harden into doctrine and keep the team from noticing that the task, audience, or system has changed.
failure_modes.source_quote: a helpful shortcut quietly hardens into doctrine
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit heuristic fit periodically: check whether the shortcut still matches the current task, audience, and system context — retire it when the environment has shifted.

failure_modes.text: Maslow's-hammer style frame lock can make a familiar heuristic look correct even when the current problem is genuinely novel.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test against a novel frame: when a familiar heuristic dominates, force a pass through a structurally different approach to detect what the shortcut is hiding.

failure_modes.text: Premature simplification can hide important risk, nuance, or system dynamics that the shortcut did not capture.
failure_modes.mitigation: Preserve the nuance the heuristic strips: when simplifying for usability, flag the risks, edge cases, or system dynamics that the shortcut does not capture.

premortem_questions.text: Have we identified the implicit assumptions embedded in the model we are using, and do those assumptions hold true in this unique context?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Have we identified the implicit assumptions embedded in the model we are using, and do those assumptions hold true in this unique context?

premortem_questions.text: Can I explain this solution in a single sentence to a five-year-old? If not, where exactly does my knowledge break down?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Can I explain this solution in a single sentence to a five-year-old? If not, where exactly does my knowledge break down?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough, and have we applied sufficient weight to outcomes that are one or two standard deviations from the mean?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough, and have we applied sufficient weight to outcomes that are one or two standard deviations from the mean?

heuristics.text: Start with simple summary heuristics to see the size and shape of the problem before building heavier analysis.
heuristics.source_quote: start all analytic work with simple summary statistics and heuristics

heuristics.text: Use analogies or familiar schemas as mental buckets only when they increase comprehension without hiding key differences.
heuristics.source_quote: It's like the Uber of X

heuristics.text: Apply a rule-of-three or similarly compact structure when the real need is memory, clarity, and action rather than exhaustive completeness.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready because it already distinguishes practical shortcut use from doctrinal misuse and offers explicit pre-mortem questions.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader generic heuristic catalog language because the raw file is strongest on shortcut hygiene and simple-first reconnaissance.
open_questions: The third heuristic compresses several communication examples into one practical structure-oriented intervention.

allies: The source is strong on cognitive-load-theory, communication frameworks, and hypothesis-guided problem solving.
antagonists: The raw antagonists point clearly toward critical-thinking, first-principles-thinking, and slow analytic rigor.
structured_tensions: No explicit structured tension block was preserved from the raw file.
